rawIntent: Create src/modules/employee/employee.service.ts with EmployeeService class that uses IEmployeeRepository. Include unit tests in tests/unit/modules/employee/employee.service.test.ts using Vitest (import from 'vitest'). All service methods must wrap repository calls in try/catch and rethrow or handle errors. This phase depends on src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts and src/modules/employee/employee.repository.ts from Phase 1 — read them before generating any code.

successCriteria.description: EmployeeService class exists in src/modules/employee/employee.service.ts and imports IEmployeeRepository from the repository module.
Every public method of EmployeeService that calls a repository method is wrapped in a try/catch block.
Caught errors are either rethrown or handled explicitly (no silent catch blocks).
Unit tests exist in tests/unit/modules/employee/employee.service.test.ts and import from 'vitest'.
Unit tests cover at least one error scenario per service method where the repository mock rejects, verifying the service catches and rethrows/handles the error.
The service does not directly access any database or ORM; all data operations go through the injected IEmployeeRepository.

constraints: Repository pattern: All data access must go through IEmployeeRepository interface; no direct database calls.
Error handling: All repository calls must be wrapped in try/catch blocks; no unhandled promise rejections.
Audit records: If the service performs state-changing operations, it must ensure audit records are written (via repository or dedicated audit mechanism).
No sensitive data in logs: The service must not log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
Unit tests must use Vitest (import from 'vitest') and mock the repository.
The service must not import or depend on modules outside the allowed dependency direction (leave -> employee is allowed, but employee must not depend on leave, policy, balance, or notification).
outOfScope: Modifying or regenerating employee.model.ts or employee.repository.ts (these are Phase 1 artifacts, read-only).
Creating any other files beyond employee.service.ts and its unit test.
Implementing API endpoints, routes, or controllers.
Any other modules (leave, policy, balance, notification).

ambiguities.description: What specific methods should EmployeeService expose? The intent only says 'uses IEmployeeRepository' but does not list method signatures.
options: Mirror all methods from IEmployeeRepository (e.g., findById, findAll, create, update, delete).
Expose only a subset of repository methods relevant to the employee domain (e.g., getEmployee, updateEmployee, listEmployees).
Methods are defined by the architecture spec for the employee module (to be inferred from employee.model.ts and employee.repository.ts).

ambiguities.description: How should the service handle caught errors: rethrow after logging, or return a result object with success/error status?
options: Rethrow the error after logging (or wrapping in a domain-specific error).
Return a result object (e.g., { success: boolean, data?: T, error?: string }) without throwing.

ambiguities.description: What test scenarios are required beyond error handling? Should tests cover all repository methods, edge cases, or only the happy path?
options: Test all public methods with both success and error cases.
Test only the error handling paths as explicitly required by the diagnosis.
Test a representative sample of methods to demonstrate the pattern.
